# Quoin

> A semantic vocabulary for the patterns every website needs. Quoin is a CSS-replacement design language: write `<section data-pattern="hero-section" data-variant="type-only">` instead of a 12-class utility soup. 18 patterns, declared anatomy, ARIA contracts and microstates baked in, aesthetic swappable.

Quoin treats markup like type. Each pattern is a slot-and-variant contract — `hero-section` has 6 mandatory slots (`eyebrow / headline / subhead / actions / meta` + the section itself) plus 5 conditional slots gated by `data-variant`. The visual identity lives in a separable aesthetic layer; the same markup renders correctly across vellum, terminal, broadsheet, arcade, or any 2026-era aesthetic pack.

For AI coding tools generating Quoin markup at scale: this file is the index. Read `/llms-full.txt` for the full anatomy of every pattern (slot names, attribute enums, ARIA contracts, composition lineage). Read `/registry.json` for the shadcn-registry-compatible enumeration suitable for tool-config wiring.

## Authoring conventions (read these first)

- **Naming.** Every pattern's primitives use the short form `<pattern-name>-<slot>`. The hero pack's section is `data-pattern="hero-section"`. Never `data-pattern="pattern-hero-section"` (the long form is rejected by the bootstrap-integrity gate — v3.G.15). Slot names are short (`eyebrow`, `headline`, `subhead`, `actions`, `meta`), not redundant with the pattern name.
- **Variants.** Patterns with multiple aesthetic-only flavors expose them via `data-variant="..."`. Conditional slots — slots that exist only for some variants — are gated by the variant value. Example: hero-controls slot exists only when `data-variant="video"` (and is WCAG 2.2.2 mandatory there).
- **Alignment.** Content alignment uses `data-alignment="centered | left | right | split-anchor"` universally. Variant-specific axes use their own typed attributes: `data-layout` (brand-photo hero), `data-video-mode` (video hero), `data-palette` (gradient-mesh hero), `data-motion-mode` (animated hero), `data-kind` (sub-slot kind: pulse / drift / disclaimer / etc.). The legacy `data-register` attribute is deprecated and rejected by the gate (v3.G.16).
- **Composition.** Patterns that need buttons consume `class="action-button"` from `@quoin/pattern-button-system` directly; they do not re-inline button CSS under a different class name. The composition reality is enforced by the gate (v3.G.17).
- **Tokens.** Every CSS value comes from `@quoin/tokens-baseline` (175 canonical tokens across 11 DTCG 2025.10 types). Patterns do not declare raw color, spacing, type-size, or motion values — they reference token names. The token namespace is frozen at v1.0.

## Pattern catalog (15 patterns + 4 primitives)

### Foundation primitives (4)

- [prim-label](https://github.com/harrowhaus/quoin/blob/main/02_reference-packs/primitives/label/README.md): label primitive consolidating badge / status-pill / tag / chip / dismissible contracts. `class="label" data-role="badge | status | tag | chip | dismissible" data-tone="neutral | accent | success | warning | critical | info | new | beta"`. Phase 22 Cons. 5.
- [prim-sequence](https://github.com/harrowhaus/quoin/blob/main/02_reference-packs/primitives/sequence/README.md): sequence primitive (breadcrumb / ordered-list / unordered-list / sidebar-vertical). `class="sequence" data-role="..." data-separator="chevron | slash | dot | pipe | number | none"`. Phase 22 Cons. 6.
- [prim-decoration-overlay](https://github.com/harrowhaus/quoin/blob/main/02_reference-packs/primitives/decoration-overlay/README.md): positioned-absolute decorative layer (grain / texture / pattern / gradient). 5% LCP impact maximum (v3.G.14). Forward-looking for Boeing Watch + future aesthetic packs. Phase 22 Cons. 7.
- [prim-searchable-list](https://github.com/harrowhaus/quoin/blob/main/02_reference-packs/primitives/searchable-list/README.md): combobox + listbox search-and-filter primitive. 3 variants (command-palette / autocomplete-results / filter-dropdown). Composes form-fields (required) + prim-label (optional). Phase 22 Cons. 8.

### Patterns (15)

Each link below opens the canonical specimen rendered live (raw.githack.com). The specimen is the source of truth for the pattern's anatomy.

### Application surface (8)

- [button-system](https://raw.githack.com/harrowhaus/quoin/main/02_reference-packs/patterns/button-system/examples/index.html): 5 intents × 4 sizes × 8 microstates. `<button class="action-button" data-intent="primary | secondary | ghost | destructive | link" data-size="xs | sm | md | lg">`. Renders as `<a>` when `href` is supplied. 44×44 hit target floor on `sm/md/lg`.
- [data-table](https://raw.githack.com/harrowhaus/quoin/main/02_reference-packs/patterns/data-table/examples/index.html): sortable, filterable, paginated. Selection (single + multi), bulk actions, inline editing, density toggle. Sort indicators are `aria-sort`; row selection is `aria-checked` on a leading checkbox.
- [empty-state](https://raw.githack.com/harrowhaus/quoin/main/02_reference-packs/patterns/empty-state/examples/index.html): `data-variant="empty | filtered-empty | error | forbidden"` × `data-size="sm | md | lg"`. Promoted from data-table's empty cell; consumed by multiple container patterns.
- [form-fields](https://raw.githack.com/harrowhaus/quoin/main/02_reference-packs/patterns/form-fields/examples/index.html): text / email / number / select / textarea / checkbox / radio. Default / focus / disabled / readonly / error states. 33 declared tokens, 8 microstates, 4 field-level states.
- [form-validation](https://raw.githack.com/harrowhaus/quoin/main/02_reference-packs/patterns/form-validation/examples/index.html): inline error display + field-level + form-level error summary. Live-region announcement on submit; per-field error linkage via `aria-describedby`.
- [modal-dialog](https://raw.githack.com/harrowhaus/quoin/main/02_reference-packs/patterns/modal-dialog/examples/index.html): focus trap, ESC-to-close, scrim click, scroll lock. WCAG 2.4.3 (focus order) + 2.4.11 (focus not obscured) + 1.3.1 (semantic dismissal).
- [toast-notifier](https://raw.githack.com/harrowhaus/quoin/main/02_reference-packs/patterns/toast-notifier/examples/index.html): stacked toasts, auto-dismiss (timed), action affordance, live-region announcement. `role="status"` for info; `role="alert"` for critical.
- [page-header](https://raw.githack.com/harrowhaus/quoin/main/02_reference-packs/patterns/page-header/examples/index.html): breadcrumb + title + status badges + primary action + overflow actions. Sticky variant + status-badge composition.

### Marketing surface (7)

- [hero](https://raw.githack.com/harrowhaus/quoin/main/02_reference-packs/patterns/hero/examples/type-only.html): unified hero pack with 5 variants. `data-variant="type-only | animated | gradient-mesh | brand-photo | video"`. Mandatory slots: section / eyebrow / headline / subhead / actions / meta. Conditional slots gated by variant: accent (animated), background (gradient-mesh), media + overlay (brand-photo + video), controls (video, WCAG 2.2.2 mandatory).
- [feature-grid](https://raw.githack.com/harrowhaus/quoin/main/02_reference-packs/patterns/feature-grid/examples/index.html): `data-layout="three-up | four-tile | six-tile | bento | steps"` × 4 cell variants × 6 grid-level states.
- [footer-mega](https://raw.githack.com/harrowhaus/quoin/main/02_reference-packs/patterns/footer-mega/examples/index.html): `data-variant="minimal | compact | default | mega"`. Mega variant ships newsletter primitive (composes form-fields + button-system) and locale picker.
- [pricing-tiers](https://raw.githack.com/harrowhaus/quoin/main/02_reference-packs/patterns/pricing-tiers/examples/index.html): 3-tier comparison with monthly/annual toggle, per-tier feature list, per-tier CTA. "Popular" badge on featured tier.
- [stat-card](https://raw.githack.com/harrowhaus/quoin/main/02_reference-packs/patterns/stat-card/examples/index.html): numeric value + label + optional trend indicator + optional sparkline slot. Tabular numerics enforced.
- [testimonial](https://raw.githack.com/harrowhaus/quoin/main/02_reference-packs/patterns/testimonial/examples/index.html): `data-variant="compact | default | featured"`. Composes avatar-stack from vocab-app-shell.

### Navigation (1 — unified)

- [nav](https://raw.githack.com/harrowhaus/quoin/main/02_reference-packs/patterns/nav/examples/marketing.html): unified nav pack with 4 variants. `data-variant="marketing | app-chrome | docs | editorial"`. Mandatory slots: section + brand. Conditional slots (17 total) gated by variant: primary-links, item, dropdown-panel, secondary-actions, mobile-toggle (marketing + app-chrome); workspace-switcher, search, avatar, subnav (app-chrome); skip-link, topbar, sidebar, sidebar-section, meta-actions (docs); utility, masthead, sections, search-inline, subscribe-cta (editorial). Composes button-system + form-fields + modal-dialog as peer packs.

## How to use Quoin from generated code

Three integration paths, ordered by maturity.

### Static (today, ready)

For prototypes and demos: copy the canonical specimen HTML file directly. Each specimen is self-contained — markup + inline tokens + per-pattern CSS in one HTML document. The specimens render byte-faithfully without any build step.

The CDN-loadable canonical tokens file:

```
https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/harrowhaus/quoin@main/02_reference-packs/tokens-baseline/tokens.css
```

### shadcn-style (today, via /registry.json)

For projects using the shadcn-CLI or shadcn-MCP tooling: add Quoin's registry as a peer:

```json
{
  "registries": {
    "@quoin": "https://raw.githubusercontent.com/harrowhaus/quoin/main/registry.json"
  }
}
```

Then `npx shadcn@latest add @quoin/hero` lands the pattern in your project's components/quoin/ tree.

### Compiler-driven (v1.0 launch, in progress)

The Quoin compiler (TypeScript, Vite plugin, ESM) consumes a token pack + impl pack + pattern packs and emits CSS at build time. Production path for v1.0+. Compiler tests currently green at 96/96; launch staged in `05_launch/`.

## Halt conditions for AI generating Quoin markup

If you (the AI tool) generate markup that violates these, the bootstrap-integrity gate will fail at PR time and a human will reject the PR. Save the reviewer's time by avoiding:

- `data-pattern="pattern-X-Y"` (the long `pattern-` prefix). Use `data-pattern="X-Y"`.
- `data-pattern` values with double-dash separators. Use single-dash.
- `data-register=` on any slot in the hero pack (or any future pack that's consolidated). Use `data-alignment`, `data-video-mode`, `data-kind`, etc. per the slot.
- Re-implementing button styles under a `.cta` class instead of using `class="action-button"`.
- Hardcoded color, spacing, type-size, or motion values. Reference token names (`var(--accent)`, `var(--space-4)`, etc.).
- `<h1>` in any slot other than `hero-headline`. Marketing pages have exactly one h1.
- Hero variant=video without a `<button data-pattern="hero-controls">` slot. WCAG 2.2.2 mandatory.

## Architectural locks (the v3.G.\* series)

Six locks ship with Phase 22 Consolidation 3 — see [`PHASE_GATES.md`](PHASE_GATES.md) for the full text:

- v3.G.15 — `data-pattern` naming convention (programmatic)
- v3.G.16 — `data-register` deprecation per consolidation (programmatic)
- v3.G.17 — composition reality (programmatic)
- v3.G.18 — anatomy documentation via structured-markdown tables (documented)
- v3.G.19 — pack code lives once; examples demonstrate variants (documented)
- v3.G.20 — variants in pattern packs; values in aesthetic packs (documented)

## Documentation depth

- `/llms-full.txt` — full anatomy reference for every pattern. Slot-by-slot, attribute-by-attribute. Use this when generating markup at scale.
- `/registry.json` — shadcn-registry-compatible enumeration.
- [Spec](https://github.com/harrowhaus/quoin/tree/main/00_spec) — tokens, primitives, pack format, pack types.
- [CHANGELOG.md](https://github.com/harrowhaus/quoin/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md) — every shipped change in reverse chronological order.
